paper_title: Ice Core and Climate Model Analysis

methods.description: Process for analyzing ice core samples to obtain high-resolution records of nitrate and isotopic compositions, and to date the ice core sections.

steps.description: The core was drilled in the clean air sector at Kohnen Station, DML on the high-elevation East Antarctic Plateau.
steps.searchable_summary: drill ice core at Kohnen Station, DML

steps.description: The core was analyzed for continuous flow analysis (CFA) of nitrate (NO3), sodium (Na), and magnesium (Mg) mass concentrations and electrolytic meltwater conductivity at the British Antarctic Survey (BAS), Cambridge.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CFA for nitrate, sodium, magnesium concentrations

steps.description: Discrete sections for the 15N(NO3) composition were analyzed at the Institute of Environmental Geosciences (IGE), University of Grenoble.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze 15N(NO3) composition in discrete ice core sections

steps.description: Dating was achieved by annual layer counting of measured concentrations of Na and Mg following previous studies at DML.
steps.searchable_summary: date ice core using annual layer counting of Na and Mg concentrations

methods.description: Description of the coupled chemistry-climate model used, including updates and changes made to simulate atmospheric conditions and reactions.

steps.description: The model consists of the UKCA module together with the UK Met Office Unified Model, with prescribed sea ice and sea surface temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: configure UKCA and UM with prescribed sea ice and SST

steps.description: The model includes the aerosol scheme, GLOMAP-mode, to simulate the direct and indirect radiative effects.
Stratospheric ozone concentrations are determined by sets of photochemical reactions and catalytic cycles.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate stratospheric ozone with photochemical reactions

steps.description: We add eight new heterogeneous reactions to the model involving chlorine and bromine species.
steps.searchable_summary: add heterogeneous reactions for chlorine and bromine

methods.description: Validation of the model changes by simulating historical and volcanic events to compare with observed data.

steps.description: The changes to the stratospheric chemistry are tested by running the model for 30 years in a year 2000 time slice experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: run 30-year model simulation for year 2000 validation

steps.description: To assess the model response to a volcanic perturbation, we simulate the eruption of Mount Pinatubo.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate Mount Pinatubo eruption for model validation

methods.description: Investigation of the effects of explosive volcanoes on the stratosphere using preindustrial forcings and a series of volcanic perturbation runs.

steps.description: Using the CMIP6 preindustrial forcings, a year 1850 control run is produced.
steps.searchable_summary: produce year 1850 control run with preindustrial forcings

steps.description: The effect from explosive volcanoes is investigated by running a series of four volcanic perturbation runs.
